body.title: tree_index seed references deleted PROC_READING_FEEDER facia (4 test failures)
body.result: Chose option 2: found PROC_READING_FEEDER_V2 from D334 commit 6627ae1f (seed_reading_facia.py). Updated tree_index.py _SPECIAL_TREES to point at the string-id replacement. test_tree_index.py: 18/18 pass. Full suite: 2170 passed, 0 failed.
body.description:
Discovered in 2026-04-13 day-close audit.

SYMPTOM: 4 test failures in tests/test_tree_index.py (test_list_all_contains_seeded, test_trees_at_facia_node, test_seed_returns_8_trees, test_seed_idempotent). All fail with:

  psycopg2.errors.ForeignKeyViolation: insert or update on table 'trees' violates foreign key constraint 'trees_facia_id_fkey'
  DETAIL: Key (facia_id)=(20260327162240000000) is not present in table 'memories'.

ROOT CAUSE: wild_igor/igor/memory/tree_index.py:266 hardcodes facia_id='20260327162240000000' for the 'reading_pipeline' well-known tree, pointing to what used to be the PROC_READING_FEEDER habit memory. That memory no longer exists in the Igor-wild-0001 database (verified: zero rows in memories with that id, no PROCEDURAL memories matching '%READING_FEEDER%' at all).

Not caused by 2026-04-13 work — git log shows no recent changes to tree_index.py or test_tree_index.py. The habit was removed at some point (possibly during D334 unified-reading-tool refactor 6627ae1f) and the tree_index seed was never updated. The tests were presumably passing on another DB snapshot and drifted.

FIX OPTIONS (pick one after reading the D334 commit and any habit rewrite notes):

1. UPDATE the hardcoded facia_id in _SPECIAL_TREES to point at whatever replaced PROC_READING_FEEDER in the unified reading tool. Preferred if there's a clear replacement habit.

2. REMOVE 'reading_pipeline' from _SPECIAL_TREES entirely if the unified reading tool doesn't use a tree-anchor habit anymore. Update test_seed_returns_8_trees to expect 7 instead of 8. Cleanest if the reading pipeline is no longer tree-structured.

3. MAKE seed_well_known_trees defensive: check facia existence before creating the tree entry, skip with a log warning if missing. Lowest-risk but masks future drift.

RECOMMENDATION: option 1 or 2 depending on what the unified reading tool actually uses. Do NOT just make it defensive — we want this drift caught, not hidden.

SCOPE: single-file code change + test expectation update. Should be ~20 minutes once the right replacement facia_id is identified.

INERTIA: LOW. memory/tree_index.py is a seed module, not a hot path.
